# A New Blog, New Job, and ()

(Intro, sections, photos, book club?)

(Intro) Hello! Welcome to the very first of many blog posts

These first few weeks will be a sort of test run before I head off to the UK in September, so expect format and content to change every week until then as I work out the kinks.

On the **19th**, I officially began my summer job as a production assistant for Early Music Vancouver's (EMV) summer festival. My office for the day was UBC's Old Aud, where I was in charge of setting up and providing refreshments to the cast of Orfeo (the opera EMV is producing as part of this year's summer festival) as they rehearsed.

During my lunch break, I tried to watch the World Cup final, but the only place on UBC where I could properly watch it was fully booked. I ultimately had to rely on iShowSpeed's livestream to get a picture of the match, and I did manage to catch Spain's winning goal against Argentina.

Since I already brought in the majority of refreshments during my first day, I only needed to add the items I bought since then while setting up on the **20th**, so the entire process was far simpler. However, I also helped to move the harpsichord and organ needed for Orfeo into the Chan Centre from the truck they were being transported in. While moving the harpsichord was easy enough, though it needed to be done rather delicately due to how fragile it was, moving the organ required the combined efforts of six people to accomplish, mainly because it weighted 600lbs.

After I got off work, I gave in to my urges and purchased the maple syrup can hoodie I saw at the UBC bookstore when I looked around during my lunch break. Since it was a clearance item, buying it was a no-brainer.

For the **21st**, I helped set up and strike EMV's free event at Granville island, which included the sound system being used. Since this process also involved transporting equipment to and from EMV's office nearby, with the help of a rental van, this day ended up being the day I was most involved with my job so far. Still, I got to listen to some amazing early music and watch some Baroque dancing along the way, all in the atmosphere of Granville Island, so I'm not complaining.

(As a side effect of being on Granville Island, I was unable to set up refreshments for the Orfeo rehearsal. I hope the cast amde do without their usual service.)

Book Club:

I started reading **Values** by Mark Carney a few weeks ago. I want to understand the direction Canada is headed in, and where better to start? Att least he has a plan, even if I disagree with some parts of it.
